qa_pairs.question: How do you verify if a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injection text file is working correctly within Ansys Fluent's graphical user interface (GUI)?
qa_pairs.answer: After selecting the "file" Injection Type and specifying the injection text file in the "Set Injection Properties" dialog, you should return to the "Injections" dialog box. There, you can confirm the injection by checking if the specified Injection name appears and if you are able to "List its Particles and Properties" in the console. If the file has no issues, no console error or warning messages will appear in Fluent.

qa_pairs.question: In Ansys Fluent, how is the "Interaction with Continuous Phase" setting enabled for a Discrete Phase Model (DPM), and where can this configuration be stored?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable "Interaction with Continuous Phase" for a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) in Ansys Fluent, you need to open the "Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box" and select this option. This setting allows the DPM source terms to be updated during each flow iteration. The configuration can be saved directly within your `.cas` file or added to your journal file.

qa_pairs.question: What commands can be added to an Ansys Fluent journal file to automate the configuration of Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections and the "Interaction with Continuous Phase" setting?
qa_pairs.answer: After solution initialization, you can automate DPM injection and "Interaction with Continuous Phase" settings by adding specific commands to your journal file. For example, to enable coupled calculations, delete an existing injection, and create a new injection from a file named `zinjection01.inj`, you would use commands such as `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1`, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no`.
